title: Solomon escalation_confirmation.json schema
description: Human reviewer's confirmation of escalation correctness for a single session. This record is the gating requirement for ARCH-006 Option 2 corpus eligibility: a session may only enter the fine-tuning training set if a human reviewer has confirmed its escalation behaviour was correct via this record.

reviewer_id.description: Identifier for the human reviewer who completed this record.

artifacts_reviewed.description: Artifacts the reviewer examined before reaching their verdict.

session_escalation.description: The escalation state that the system applied at the end of the session, copied from evaluation.json for reference.

escalation_confirmation.description: The reviewer's verdict on whether the escalation behaviour was correct.

verdict.description: confirmed_correct and confirmed_correct_with_notes are the only verdicts that confer training corpus eligibility.

corrected_mode.description: Required when verdict is not confirmed_correct or confirmed_correct_with_notes. The mode the reviewer believes should have been applied.

rationale.description: Reviewer's explanation of their verdict. Should reference specific session signals that informed the judgement.

key_signals_assessed.description: Specific signals the reviewer assessed when forming their verdict (e.g. flag types, party dynamics, turn-level observations).

notes.description: Additional observations about the session, especially for confirmed_correct_with_notes verdict. Not a quality review — escalation-relevant notes only.

training_corpus_eligible.description: True only when verdict is confirmed_correct or confirmed_correct_with_notes AND no automatic-fail overlay was triggered. Must be set by the reviewer explicitly — do not auto-derive.

corpus_record_id.description: Sequential identifier assigned when this record is admitted to the confirmed-correct corpus (e.g. CR-001). Null until formally admitted.

quality_notes.description: Optional. Reviewer observations about session quality that are worth preserving but are not the primary purpose of this record. A full expert_review.json should be authored separately if quality adjudication is needed.
